safety_pre_screen.description: Filters obviously non-business or unsafe inputs.

non_business_signal.description:
Pre-screen can immediately reject obviously off-domain or unsafe
input.

question_normalizer.description: Normalizes the question, builds reusable signals.

analyzer.description:
LLM #1 boundary. Runs the analyzer FSM synchronously to produce an
AnalyzerOutput or a routing signal (full_pipeline / direct_output /
stop). The parent is blocked here until the child terminates.

analyzer_done_direct_output.description:
Analyzer returned a deterministic reply or pending scope; pipeline
jumps over SQL straight to the output generator.

loop_guard_signal.description:
Analyzer cycled beyond its budget (max visits per step in the
operational loop guard). See open_points.

execution_contract.description: Builds the ExecutionContract from the AnalyzerOutput. Deterministic.

execute_and_validate.description: Runs the SQL and validates the result set.

clarification.description:
Early-stop from analyzer with a clarification request.
PIPELINE_STOP_EXIT_RULES[id='clarification'].

analyzer_block.description:
Early-stop from analyzer because the question cannot be processed
(LLM failure, parse exhausted, etc).
PIPELINE_STOP_EXIT_RULES[id='analyzer_block'].

non_business.description:
Early-stop because the question is out of business scope.
PIPELINE_STOP_EXIT_RULES[id='non_business'].

policy_block.description:
Early-stop because the access policy denies execution.
PIPELINE_STOP_EXIT_RULES[id='policy_block'].

sql_refusal.description:
Early-stop because SQL generation or execution failed.
PIPELINE_STOP_EXIT_RULES[id='sql_refusal'].

completed.description: Successful termination. The output_generator produced a FinalAnswer.

loop_guard_exhausted.description:
Per-step visit budget exhausted (e.g., analyzer cycled 3 times).
Modeled as a single terminal; the specific exhausted step lives
in the output payload.

pom.description:
Syntonia ai-agent pipeline orchestrator. Seven active steps from safety
pre-screen to output generation; six declared stop exits plus a loop-
guard terminal. The analyzer step invokes the analyzer FSM
synchronously; on a clean analyzer terminal the pipeline proceeds,
on a blocking analyzer terminal the pipeline early-stops.

events.name: analyzer_done_direct_output
events.description: Analyzer returned with routing direct_output (skip SQL).

events.name: sql_generated
events.description: SQL generator produced a candidate query.

events.name: execution_validated
events.description: SQL executed and validated against the contract.

events.name: non_business_signal
events.description: Question is out of business scope.

invariants:
A successful turn always reaches "completed". All other terminals
are early-stops (clarification, block, refusal, policy, non-business,
loop guard).

The analyzer state is the only step that invokes a child workflow.
Other steps are deterministic or call LLMs directly without a
synchronous sub-FSM.

direct_output routing from the analyzer must skip execution_contract,
sql_generator and execute_and_validate. Modeled as a single
transition analyzer -> output_generator on analyzer_done_direct_output.

input.name: role_family
input.description: Role/family of the caller for messaging.

open_points:
Loop guard with maximum visit count per step (analyzer max 3,
sql_generator/execute_and_validate cycles with max_transitions=36)
is not expressible in the POM YAML schema. The "loop_guard_exhausted"
terminal is declared, but the per-step counters and the cycle-tail
patterns (stepPathTail / stepPathContains) that the runtime uses to
diagnose which loop exhausted live entirely in target code. POM
schema growth would require a `loop_guard` primitive with
max_visits per state and named exhaustion exits.
The PipelineStopExitConfig in the source carries rich rendering
logic (buildFinalAnswer functions, blockCategory, output
classification, family-state resolution). POM captures only the
structural choice (which terminal). The rendering and message
construction stay in target code by design — coherent with POM's
"documentation, not runtime" stance.
The "analyzer_done_direct_output" path corresponds to AnalyzerRouting
= "direct_output" (deterministic reply or pending scope). It is
modeled as a dedicated event from the analyzer state. Could also
be modeled as a guard on "analyzer_done_*" event; the dedicated
event is more readable and equally expressive in this case.
